master_row.title: Bayesian Evidence Accumulation Framework
master_row.canonical_statement: Hypotheses in the TZP framework are evaluated using Bayesian inference, where evidence accumulates via likelihood ratios and simpler models are preferred.
master_row.canonical_equation: \frac{P(H_{i}\mid D)}{P(H_{j}\mid D)} = \frac{P(D\mid H_{i})}{P(D\mid H_{j})} \frac{P(H_{i})}{P(H_{j})} || B_{ij} = \frac{P(D\mid H_{i})}{P(D\mid H_{j})}
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an algebraic definition relating P, D, H_i, H_j, B_ij.
master_row.dictionary: When comparing competing Trawinistic hypotheses (H_i), the posterior odds satisfy (P(H_i D)P(H_j D) = P(D H_i)P(D H_j) P(H_i)P(H_j)), with likelihoods (P(D H_i)) computed using the Trawinistic action and priors chosen to penalise complexity.
master_row.encyclopedia: This equation formalizes evidence accumulation: posterior odds equal likelihood ratio times prior odds. Interpretive role: This framework gives the registry a disciplined way to absorb new evidence. It also links the physical canon to a publication workflow, because entries can be strengthened, weakened, or retired by explicit posterior updates.
